_note: ADR-0135 intake. Three intertwined pieces decomposed as ONE change: A (hoist SettingsSchema pure types + pure guards to @agent-ui/shared), B (A2UI live-agent config as a real SettingsSchema instance + a fail-closed resolver into ProduceOptions), C (break system-prompt.ts / mini-skills.ts hardcoded prompt text into ./prompts/*.md, loaded via readFileSync at module load). Encodes the RECOMMENDED fork resolutions; forks are ruled in the ADR, none self-ratified.

nodes.label: shared/src/settings-schema.ts — pure SettingsSchema types + pure guards (findField/initialValuesFor/sanitizeNumber/sanitizeSelect), zero-dep, DOM-free
shared/src/index.ts barrel — `export type * from './settings-schema.ts'` (first TS export from the '.' surface)
app/.../settings/schema.ts — slimmed to the DOM/components-coupled control registry; imports the type from @agent-ui/shared and RE-EXPORTS it so app's existing type consumers are byte-unchanged
app/.../agent-admin/agent-admin-schema.ts — imports the hoisted guards from @agent-ui/shared and re-exports them, keeping agent-admin.ts's callers unchanged (Fork 2 = hoist)

a2ui/tools/agent/agent-config-schema.ts — liveAgentConfigSchema(providers): SettingsSchema builder; static mode/k/maxRounds/miniSkillCap fields + a model select whose options project from the passed ProvidersConfig
a2ui/tools/agent/agent-config-schema.ts — resolveProduceOptions(read, schema): ProduceOptions, reading a SettingsStore-shaped source live at call time, fail-closed via the shared guards

produce.ts — ProduceOptions gains `miniSkillCap?: number`; the selectMiniSkills call reads `opts.miniSkillCap ?? DEFAULT_MINI_SKILL_CAP` (additive, absent ⇒ byte-identical behavior; signature unchanged)

Node shell seam — readFileSync(providers.json) + validateProvidersConfig → liveAgentConfigSchema(config): the pure-core/Node-shell split (ADR-0062), reusing providers-config.ts, no parallel model list
prompts/grammar.md — the WHOLE GRAMMAR literal as ONE file (never pre-sliced fragments)
prompts/{honesty-floor,clarify-specific,negotiate-specific,ask-archetypes-specific,clarify-blue-sky,ask-archetypes-blue-sky}.md — the six standalone mode consts, one file each
prompts/negotiate-blue-sky.md — the STATIC prose through the 'Calibration examples:' header; the dynamic bullets are appended in code (trailing append, not a mid-file placeholder)

prompts/mini-skills/*.md — six per-mini-skill files, `---
id:
triggers:
---` frontmatter + markdown body (this repo's SKILL.md shape reference, simplified)
prompts/frontmatter.ts — a minimal Node-only frontmatter parser (first `---`…`---` block → single-line key:value pairs + body); no reusable one exists in-repo (confirmed)

system-prompt.ts — load prompt files via readFileSync(new URL('./prompts/…', import.meta.url)) at module load; slice INTRO_AND_NOTE/OUTPUT_RULES from the LOADED grammar; keep assertMarkersHold(); grammarFor composes the loaded consts; NEGOTIATE_BLUE_SKY appends calibrationExampleBullet output; condense the ADR header to a one-line index
mini-skills.ts — readFileSync the prompts/mini-skills dir + parseFrontmatter each file → MINI_SKILLS: MiniSkill[]; selectMiniSkills / token-budget unchanged
equivalence gate — assert the default/absent-mode prompt AND every MINI_SKILLS body is byte-identical to the pre-refactor constants (GRAMMAR byte-identity, generalized to the file-loaded bodies)
prompt-drift.test.ts stays green + unmodified — buildSystemPrompt's signature is unchanged by B and C, so the catalog-derived SET-EQUAL gate is untouched (orthogonality holds)

actions.label: define the SettingsSchema pure types once, at the DAG bottom
expose those types to app AND a2ui through one import surface
keep app's existing type consumers (settings.ts/generate.ts/validate.ts/agent-admin-schema.ts) working unchanged
keep the DOM/components-coupled control registry in app (unmoved)
provide ONE shared fail-closed guard + seed-helper implementation for both app and a2ui (Fork 2)
keep agent-admin's guard consumers working unchanged after the guards move
model the live-agent config (mode/model/k/maxRounds/miniSkillCap) as a SettingsSchema instance
source the model select options from the real providers.json registry — single source, no parallel list
read a store's values into ProduceOptions, fail-closed on a bad stored value
make the mini-skill cap a configurable ProduceOptions knob (was a hardcoded module constant)
keep produce() backward-compatible — an absent knob reproduces current behavior byte-for-byte
load GRAMMAR as one file and re-derive INTRO_AND_NOTE/OUTPUT_RULES by slicing the loaded string (byte-identity by construction preserved)
load each standalone mode-const from its own file
compose NEGOTIATE_BLUE_SKY's dynamic calibration bullets from MINI_SKILLS after loading its static prose
load the mini-skill registry from per-skill frontmatter files
parse frontmatter (id/triggers + body) with a minimal Node parser
condense the ADR-narrative header to a one-line-per-ADR index naming what each added + where it now lives
guarantee the default-mode prompt + every mini-skill body is byte-identical to the pre-refactor source
keep the catalog-drift gate (prompt-drift.test.ts) green and unmodified

edges.why: the barrel re-exports the settings-schema module
app schema.ts re-imports + re-exports the hoisted type
agent-config-schema imports the SettingsSchema type from shared
the resolver imports the hoisted fail-closed guards from shared
the Node shell builds the schema from the parsed ProvidersConfig
the resolver targets the ProduceOptions shape, which must already carry miniSkillCap
mini-skills.ts loads the registry through the frontmatter parser
the mini-skill files are the data mini-skills.ts parses
system-prompt.ts loads the standalone mode-const files
system-prompt.ts loads the negotiate-blue-sky static prose before appending the dynamic bullets
system-prompt.ts composes NEGOTIATE_BLUE_SKY bullets from the loaded MINI_SKILLS registry
the equivalence gate runs against the loaded system prompt
the equivalence gate runs against the loaded mini-skill bodies
the drift gate exercises buildSystemPrompt after the file-load rewire
